title: Rakehell Squadron
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}}
{{Otheruses|Rakehell}}
{{Organization
|image=
|name=Rakehell Squadron
|type=
|founder=[[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]<ref name="Fury">''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]''</ref>
|leader=*Wedge Antilles<ref name="Fury" />
|subgroup=
|headquarters=*[[Endor/Legends|Endor]]<ref name="Fury" />

|locations=

{{Quote|Wedge, handpick a starfighter squadron. We'll use it to chase the shuttle to safety aboard the ''Anakin Solo'', then to support any operation against Centerpoint Station.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="Fury" />}}
'''Rakehell Squadron''' was a [[New Jedi Order|Jedi]]-aligned [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] created in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]] to aid in the uprising against [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] co-[[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]] [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], whom the Jedi Order under [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] believed was promulgating further [[Second Galactic Civil War|hostilities]] between the separatist [[Confederation]] and the Galactic Alliance. The Jedi, having previously broken with the Galactic Alliance due to Solo's status as a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], planned to launch a mission to weaken Solo's position in order to eventually overthrow him, and Rakehell Squadron was the key to their plans. Led by [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], Rakehell Squadron consisted of both [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and non-Jedi members, most of whom were veteran fighter [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]], even though one of its members, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]], was still obligated by duty to the Galactic Alliance, as he had originally been sent to the Jedi as a diplomatic Galactic Alliance envoy without the permission of Solo.

Rakehell Squadron flew in the Jedi [[Battle of Centerpoint Station (Second Galactic Civil War)|attack]] on the [[Centerpoint Station]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] and the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Anakin Solo (starship)|''Anakin Solo'']], chasing the [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] ''[[Reveille]]'' to the ''Anakin Solo'', which contained a Jedi infiltration team attempting to rescue the [[Hapan]] ''[[Chume'da]]'' [[Allana Djo Solo|Allana]], whom Solo was using as political leverage over the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]]. After the infiltration team successfully penetrated the ''Anakin Solo''{{'s}} defenses, Rakehell Squadron was assigned to draw off Galactic Alliance fighters and provide cover for the infiltration of another Jedi strike team onto Centerpoint Station aboard a second shuttle, [[Broadside (shuttle)|''Broadside'']]. This second team's objective was to destroy the station. Rakehell Squadron proved very effective in battle, managing to shoot down two members of the elite Galactic Alliance unit [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] and successfully completed all of its objectives, albeit at significant attrition rates. After covering the exfiltration of the intrusion team and Allana on the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], Rakehell Squadron withdrew to a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] near [[Gyndine/Legends|Gyndine]].

==History==
===Formation===
{{Quote|The general came here to make an official request by the GA government that the Jedi Order return to the Galactic Alliance fold, as is our sworn duty.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="Fury" />}}
Rakehell Squadron was an impromptu unit formed by [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] in response to a planned [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] strike on [[Centerpoint Station]]. After the [[New Jedi Order|Jedi Order]] had split from the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] over [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo's]] fall to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] and [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] status, Galactic Alliance co-[[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]] [[Cha Niathal]] had seen the [[Confederation]] achieve near-parity with the Galactic Alliance militarily. While the balance of power in the war had been against the separatist Confederation early in the war, several losses and the departure of the Jedi Order had weakened the Galactic Alliance. Niathal had grown less and less confident in the abilities and judgment of Solo and wanted to hasten the end of the [[Second Galactic Civil War]]. Knowing that returning the Jedi Order to the fold would greatly enhance her ability to end the war and also knowing that co-Chief of State Jacen Solo had broken the ties with and set the Galactic Alliance against the Jedi in the first place, Niathal discreetly dispatched [[General/Legends|General]] [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]] to make contact with the Jedi Order in exile.<ref name="Fury" />

Celchu, accompanied by his aides, [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Toval Seyah]] and starfighter pilot [[Syal Antilles]], made contact with the Jedi Order and their affiliates, including [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and Celchu's old [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] leader and Syal's father, Wedge Antilles, on their refuge on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. Celchu discussed Niathal's request with the head of the Jedi Order, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], after being granted an audience, phrasing his statements in such a way that Niathal's involvement could not be traced back to her should Celchu's mission be compromised, as per Niathal's instructions. Skywalker refused Celchu's request, on the grounds that he refused to place the Jedi subject to Jacen Solo.<ref name="Fury" />

Instead, Skywalker stated that the immediate goals of the Jedi were to find a new location for their refuge—Celchu's presence and eventual report would compromise their presence on Endor's moon—recover the kidnapped [[Allana Djo Solo|Allana]], the heir of the [[Hapes Cluster/Legends|Hapes Cluster]], who was being held prisoner as political and personal leverage over [[Hapan]] [[Queen Mother]] [[Tenel Ka Djo|Tenel Ka]], and to destroy Centerpoint Station with the aid of Toval Seyah, an expert on the station, to prevent its use by either the Confederation or Galactic Alliance.<ref name="Fury" /> The station had been used decades earlier to destroy several [[Star/Legends|stars]],<ref name="The New Essential Chronology">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> and Skywalker wanted it disabled, to prevent it being used as a weapon of war. To gain a team of Jedi entrance to Solo's personal [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial'' II-class Star Destroyer]] [[Anakin Solo (starship)|''Anakin Solo'']], Skywalker planned on commandeering Celchu's [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] for use as an infiltration vehicle—it contained the necessary codes to grant clearance to land in the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] of the ''Anakin Solo''.<ref name="Fury" />

To say the least, Celchu was displeased by this idea, as his duty was first to the Galactic Alliance and he saw little that the Galactic Alliance would gain from the Jedi plan, despite his own personal feelings in favor of the Jedi. However, Wedge Antilles was able to secure his cooperation by pointing an imaginary [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] at Celchu. As Celchu could now justifiably say he was coerced if questioned by his superiors, he agreed to Skywalker's plan. His long ties with many members of the Jedi Order and friendship with Jedi supporters, including Antilles, Solo, and the [[Horn family|family]] of old Rogue Squadron pilot and Jedi Master [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] influenced his decision as well. Celchu furthermore managed to persuade Antilles to let him accompany the Jedi strike team in a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] as a means of returning him to the Galactic Alliance. Also, Celchu appointed Syal Antilles as the official diplomatic liaison from the Galactic Alliance to the Jedi Order, allowing her freedom from the same oaths of duty that constrained him from fully working with Antilles and the Jedi. With that matter settled, Skywalker planned to have Antilles raise a squadron of starfighters to chase Celchu's shuttle and the infiltration team it would contain into the ''Anakin Solo'' and then provide assistance in the mission to destroy Centerpoint Station.<ref name="Fury" />

Antilles carried out his orders, forming Rakehell Squadron out of a combination of personal friends, members of the Jedi Order, and his own daughter Syal Antilles. The squadron, supplied with [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-Wing starfighters]] by the Jedi Order, was initially stationed on the pleasure ship and converted ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer ''[[Errant Venture/Legends|Errant Venture]]'' until word came that Jacen Solo was leading an assault fleet to the [[Corellian system/Legends|Corellian system]]. The time had come for Skywalker and Antilles to make their play.<ref name="Fury" />

Launching from a point two [[Light-year/Legends|light-years]] outside of the Corellian [[Star system/Legends|system]], Rakehell Squadron made a short jump through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], escorting a pair of shuttles. The first, ''Reveille'', was to be chased into the Galactic Alliance fleet by the Rakehells to allow its passengers, Luke Skywalker, Han Solo, [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], Jedi Master [[Saba Sebatyne]], [[Ben Skywalker]], [[Iella Wessiri Antilles]], and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] to infiltrate Jacen Solo's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] and rescue Allana. The second shuttle, ''Broadside'', would carry another force of Jedi including [[Kyp Durron]], [[Jaden Korr]], [[Valin Horn]], and [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] Seyah, all disguised as [[Galactic Alliance Guard]] troopers, to Centerpoint Station for their attempt to destroy the [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]].<ref name="Fury" />

===Battle of Centerpoint Station===
[[File:Centerpoint_Station.jpg|thumb|left|Centerpoint Station.]]
{{Quote|Rakehell Leader to squadron. Break by wing pairs whenever you feel like it—or when I say break.|Wedge Antilles|<ref name="Fury" />}}
As they approached the scene of the battle, where [[Confederation]] forces were already engaging the Galactic Alliance, the members of Rakehell Squadron, or "Rakes," began pursuing ''Reveille'', but were firing to barely miss the craft, creating an illusion of pursuit to reinforce the idea that ''Reveille'' was friendly to Galactic Alliance forces. Flying as Rakehell Three, Tycho Celchu agreed to fly on the run towards the ''Anakin Solo'' as part of the squadron, but refrained from firing. Also, his communications board was slaved to Antilles', preventing him from providing the Galactic Alliance with information on the squadron's plans. Furthermore, ''Reveille'' was broadcasting its proper registration as a Galactic Alliance vessel, as Iella Wessiri Antilles had already [[Slicer/Legends|sliced]] the password out of its [[Computer/Legends|computer]].<ref name="Fury" />

As the shuttle and the starfighters harrowing it approached the ''Anakin Solo'', the Galactic Alliance [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] opened fire on them. The members of Rakehell Squadron went defensive, evading the fire, but Tycho Celchu took advantage of the confusion to discreetly inform Syal Antilles that he was going to make a break for the Star Destroyer's hangar bay. He also ordered her not to follow him. Following ''Reveille'' in as the other Rakehells broke off, Celchu was able to evade the Galactic Alliance fire and land safely inside its hangar, intent on alerting the security forces gathered there of the danger posed by the Jedi inside ''Reveille''. However, as he was exiting his cockpit, he was shot by a trigger-happy security guard, allowing the Jedi to exit their vessel with the element of surprise intact. Celchu would later recover and continue in Galactic Alliance service, despite his own personal misgivings.<ref name="Fury" />

Following their successful mock pursuit of ''Reveille'', Wedge Antilles directed Rakehell Squadron to commence a similar routine, this time on ''Broadside''. However, while they were chasing ''Broadside'', Rakehell Squadron found itself opposed by Galactic Alliance starfighters, and not just any unit. As they approached Centerpoint Station, the Rakehells found themselves entangled with [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], the elite starfighter squadron dating back to the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] that had also been founded and commanded by Luke Skywalker and Wedge Antilles. The two squadrons [[Starfighter combat/Legends|clashed]] in a duel prolonged by the high levels of skill present in the members of both squadrons. Initially, the Rakehells, particularly Wedge and Syal Antilles, tried to avoid destroying the starfighters of their opponents, but as the engagement continued, they had no choice. Even glancing shots intended to damage the Rogues were insufficient to stop the famous squadron and Rakehell casualties were mounting. In the midst of battle, the senior Antilles noted that while at least two Rogues, including [[Rogue Leader/Legends|Rogue Leader]], a [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] named [[Lensi]], had been [[Death/Legends|killed]], the Rakehells had suffered losses of their own. Rakehell Six, [[Twool (Rodian)|Twool]], had been killed and Rakehell Eight, [[Cheriss ke Hanadi]], was extravehicular. Additionally, Sanola Ti, flying as Rakehell Two, had also sustained damage to her starfighter and Antilles ordered her to withdraw from the engagement zone. With Celchu having returned to the Galactic Alliance, the Rakehells had only eight pilots remaining in the engagement zone. However, ''Broadside'' had safely docked with Centerpoint Station and disembarked its group of saboteurs, who were successful in initiating the destruction of the [[Space station/Legends|station]].<ref name="Fury" />

The arrival of a [[Commenor/Legends|Commenori]] task force to the battle in support of the Corellians had diverted the attention of the Galactic Alliance and drew the Rogues and other starfighter units away from the Rakehells, whose intentions the Galactic Alliance forces had been unable to ascertain. Seyah, Durron, and the other Centerpoint saboteurs withdrew on ''Broadside'' from the doomed station. Meanwhile, the Jedi aboard the ''Anakin Solo'' had successfully retrieved Allana. Knowing that they could not retreat back on ''Reveille'', Luke Skywalker had arranged for [[Jagged Fel]], [[Kyle Katarn]], and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] aboard the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] to provide extraction for the intrusion team aboard the ''Anakin Solo.'' As the ''Millennium Falcon'' approached the ''Anakin Solo'', the Rakehells were tasked with providing cover for the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]].<ref name="Fury" />

Opening fire with [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] and [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]], the Rakehells were successful in diverting the ''Anakin Solo''{{'s}} fire from the ''Millennium Falcon'' and the intrusion team was successfully evacuated, one step ahead of an enraged Jacen Solo, who ordered his [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]] to use [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]] to capture the freighter. However, they were unable to do so in time and the ''Millennium Falcon'', ''Broadside'', and the Rakehells escaped to hyperspace. Just before they left, the Rakehells observed the detonation of Centerpoint Station, which obliterated both the Corellian/Commenori fleets and the Galactic Alliance flotilla, except the ''Anakin Solo'', and brought the battle to an abrupt halt.<ref name="Fury" />

Leaving the Corellian system, the Rakehells and the ships they were escorting next stopped at an [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] station near [[Gyndine/Legends|Gyndine]] owned by [[Tendrando Arms]]. As the owners of Tendrando, [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and [[Tendra Risant Calrissian]] were sympathetic to the Jedi, there was no issue with the use of the facility. At that point, the squadron was dissolved, with the Jedi returning to their base on Endor.<ref name="Fury" />

==Organization==
As were most standard starfighter squadrons of that era, Rakehell Squadron was composed of twelve members, which were divided into three flights of four starfighters each, labeled One Flight, Two Flight, and Three Flight.<ref name="Fury" /> This was the same organization employed by Antilles in previous commands such as Rogue Squadron<ref name="Rogue Squadron">''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]''</ref> and [[Wraith Squadron]].<ref name="Wraith Squadron">''[[X-Wing: Wraith Squadron]]''</ref> In combat against the Galactic Alliance, the squadron members broke by wing pairs, with each pair of Rakehells sticking together to provide cover for his or her wingmate,<ref name="Fury" /> another standard practice among starfighter units.<ref name="I, Jedi">''[[I, Jedi]]''</ref>

==Equipment==
The equipment employed by Rakehell Squadron was supplied by the considerable resources of the Jedi Order, which was the equivalent to a sizable paramilitary force. The [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-Wing starfighter]],<ref name="Fury" /> variants of which were in use as early as [[0 BBY/Legends|forty years earlier]],<ref name="EaW">''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]''</ref> a stable workhorse of the Rebel Alliance, New Republic,<ref name="NEGVV">''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref> and Galactic Alliance starfighter corps, as well as being the preferred spacecraft of the elite Rogue Squadron for years,<ref name="Rogue Squadron" /><ref name="Betrayal">''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]''</ref> was the vehicle of choice for Rakehell Squadron.<ref name="Fury" />

Two of the Rakehell Squadron X-Wings, flown respectively by Tycho Celchu and Syal Antilles, received special modifications to their communications equipment. As they were both officers of the Galactic Alliance military, their first duty would have been to report the efforts of the Jedi Order to the Galactic Alliance. By slaving their communications equipment to his until any warning Celchu or the younger Antilles provided would be belated, Wedge Antilles removed this obstacle to their participation in the squadron's actions. Also, both Celchu and Syal Antilles willingly joined in the Rakehells and Syal even destroyed a member of Rogue Squadron after Celchu released her from her Galactic Alliance obligations.<ref name="Fury" />

==Members==
The members of Rakehell Squadron came from many different backgrounds. Most of them were former or current members of the New Republic or Galactic Alliance military.<ref name="Fury" /><ref name="Wraith Squadron" /><ref name="NEGTC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> Five of them were Jedi. All of them were already veteran starfighter pilots. Their combined experience and talent made them a group comprising some of the best starfighter pilots of their era, even if they had never flown together as a group prior to their first engagement.<ref name="Fury" />

===Wedge Antilles===
[[File:Wedge-RebelDream.jpg|thumb|right|160px|[[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], legendary starfighter pilot and leader of Rakehell Squadron.]]
{{Main|Wedge Antilles/Legends}}
A famed [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] starfighter pilot, Wedge Antilles was '''Rakehell Leader''', the leader of One Flight,<ref name="Fury" /> and a veteran of [[New Republic Starfighter Command/Legends|Starfighter Command]] since the early days of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]]. In addition to being a survivor of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|first Death Star run]], one of two men to have a claim on [[Battle of Endor/Legends|destroying]] the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]], a founding member and later commander of the famed Rogue Squadron,<ref name="NEGTC" /> Antilles had also flown in starfighters during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] with considerable success, despite having been promoted to a command-level rank.<ref name="Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand">''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand]]''</ref> With kills numbering in the hundreds,<ref name="Rogue Squadron" /> Antilles was described as the best starfighter pilot alive<ref name="Wraith Squadron" /> and was also a formidable trainer, having trained at least three of the pilots in Rakehell Squadron personally.<ref name="Fury" /><ref name="Rogue Squadron" /><ref name="Wraith Squadron" /> By the end of his service with the Galactic Alliance, Antilles had attained the rank of general.<ref name="Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand" /> Although retired at the start of the Confederation-Galactic Alliance War, Antilles soon found himself embroiled in the hostilities after escaping Galactic Alliance custody. He served as the head of the Corellian military for a while,<ref name="Betrayal" /> but an effort to remove him, followed by an assassination attempt, caused him to side with the Jedi Order,<ref name="Exile">''[[Legacy of the Force: Exile]]''</ref> which also later withdrew from the Galactic Alliance.<ref name="Inferno">''[[Legacy of the Force: Inferno]]''</ref> During the Battle of Centerpoint Station, Antilles directed the Rakehells in their missions, but also flew in combat, shooting down Rogue Leader Lensi. He survived the Battle of Centerpoint Station.<ref name="Fury" />

===Sanola Ti===
{{Main|Sanola Ti}}
Sanola Ti, a [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomiri]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], flew in Rakehell Squadron as '''Rakehell Two.''' Part of Luke Skywalker's New Jedi Order, Ti was the niece of famed Jedi Knight [[Kirana Ti]] and inherited her relative's skill in a starfighter, despite her youth compared to the other members of Rakehell Squadron. Sanola Ti survived Rakehell Squadron's participation in the Battle of Centerpoint Station, though her starfighter sustained damage and she was ordered out of the battle zone early by Wedge Antilles.<ref name="Fury" />

===Tycho Celchu===
[[File:Tychoheadshot.jpg|thumb|right|160px|[[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]].]]
{{Main|Tycho Celchu/Legends}}
Tycho Celchu carried the designation '''[[Rakehell Three]]''' during his tenure in Rakehell Squadron.<ref name="Fury" /> A native of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], Celchu originally flew for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] before the [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destruction of Alderaan]] convinced him to defect to the Rebel Alliance. Celchu went on to become an accomplished Rebel pilot in his own right, flying with Rogue Squadron along luminaries such as Wedge Antilles. He even survived the attack run on the second Death Star during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Later, Celchu became the commander of Rogue Squadron for many years, serving the Rebel Alliance and New Republic throughout the Galactic Civil War. Shortly before the war's conclusion, Celchu retired from military service after years in the starfighter cockpit, marrying his love and fellow Alderaanian, [[Winter Celchu]].<ref name="NEGTC" /> Celchu would later return to service during the Yuuzhan Vong War as a [[Colonel/Legends|colonel]].<ref name="Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand" /> Though his wife wished him to retire again after the Yuuzhan Vong War,<ref name="The Unifying Force">''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''</ref> Celchu was again in active Galactic Alliance service during the Confederation-Galactic Alliance War, then ranked as a general.<ref name="Betrayal" /> When Chief of State Cha Niathal wished to discreetly discuss the potential return of the Jedi Order to the Galactic Alliance, she dispatched Celchu to meet with his old friend and commander Wedge Antilles, who had been aiding the Jedi, as well as Luke Skywalker. Through a series of events that included Skywalker promising to return the Jedi to the Galactic Alliance after Jacen Solo had been dealt with, Celchu volunteered to fly in Rakehell Squadron, although his duty as a Galactic Alliance officer required Antilles to disable his communications to prevent Celchu from notifying his superiors about the Jedi plan for the Battle of Centerpoint Station. Celchu's participation in the battle saw him land his X-Wing in the ''Anakin Solo''{{'s}} hangar safely, though an over-reacting security guard shot him in the chest, injuring him.<ref name="Fury" />

===Syal Antilles===
{{Main|Syal Antilles}}
Syal Antilles, who flew as '''Rakehell Four''',<ref name="Fury" /> was the daughter of Wedge Antilles and Iella Wessiri Antilles.<ref name="NEGTC" /> Antilles was part of the Galactic Alliance military when the Confederation-Galactic Alliance War broke out, even flying against her father briefly in the [[Blockade of Corellia]] and [[Battle of Tralus (Second Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Tralus]]. Trained by Tycho Celchu<ref name="Betrayal" /> and Wedge Antilles, Syal was already an accomplished pilot in her own right, though it had been some time since she had flown an X-Wing as of the Battle of Centerpoint Station. Released from her duties to the Galactic Alliance by her superior, Tycho Celchu, Antilles was free to participate in completing Rakehell's objectives, though the course of the battle saw her destroy a member of Rogue Squadron, an act that she rued. Syal Antilles survived the Battle of Centerpoint Station.<ref name="Fury" />

===Corran Horn===
[[File:Corrancrop.jpg|thumb|right|160px|Corran Horn, Rakehell Five.]]
{{Main|Corran Horn/Legends}}
Corran Horn was a Corellian starfighter pilot<ref name="NEGTC" /> and Jedi Master who flew as '''Rakehell Five'''. Due to his experience, Wedge Antilles placed him in charge of Two Flight.<ref name="Fury" /> During the Galactic Civil War, Horn was already a skilled starfighter pilot when he joined the Rebellion and he was soon accepted into Rogue Squadron, where he trained and flew under the likes of Wedge Antilles and Tycho Celchu. He soon became one of the most famed members of the squadron, though he temporarily left to pursue [[Jedi training/Legends|Jedi training]], the cause of his later complete resignation from the military. The Yuuzhan Vong War saw Horn again on the front lines of battle, participating in actions such as the [[Battle of Ithor]]<ref name="NEGTC" /> and the [[Battle of Borleias (Yuuzhan Vong War; Second)|Second Battle of Borleias]]; the latter battle saw him once again fly as a member of Rogue Squadron.<ref name="Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand" /> Horn returned to the Jedi Order full-time after the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] were defeated, and became one of the leading Masters of the Order. Corran Horn survived the Battle of Centerpoint Station.<ref name="Fury" />

===Twool===
{{Main|Twool (Rodian)}}
Twool was a male [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] Jedi Knight who was assigned the position of '''Rakehell Six'''. Twool's X-Wing had been refitted with enhanced sensors for an earlier mission and these modifications were carried over to the Battle of Centerpoint Station. However, Twool perished in combat against Rogue Squadron during Rakehell's approach on Centerpoint Station.<ref name="Fury" />

===Tyria Sarkin Tainer===
[[File:Tyria2.jpg|thumb|right|160px|[[Tyria Sarkin Tainer]].]]
{{Main|Tyria Sarkin Tainer}}
Tyria Sarkin Tainer, '''Rakehell Seven''',<ref name="Fury" /> was a native of [[Toprawa/Legends|Toprawa]] and a founding member of Wraith Squadron, serving under Wedge Antilles as both a [[Commando/Legends|commando]] and an X-Wing pilot. During her time with the Wraiths, Tyria Sarkin Tainer fell in love with<ref name="Wraith Squadron" /> and married fellow Wraith [[Kell Tainer]].<ref name="Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream">''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream]]''</ref> While she had been aware of her mild [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]] prior to her service in the New Republic military,<ref name="Wraith Squadron" /> Tainer later resigned from service to pursue Jedi training, like Corran Horn. Unlike Horn, though, she followed the path of the Jedi on her own, until Luke Skywalker eventually confirmed her as a full-fledged Knight prior to the Yuuzhan Vong War.<ref name="Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream" /> Tainer survived the Battle of Centerpoint Station.<ref name="Fury" />

===Cheriss ke Hanadi===
{{Main|Cheriss ke Hanadi}}
Cheriss ke Hanadi was a female [[Adumar/Legends|Adumari]] whom Wedge Antilles met during the New Republic's inaugural diplomatic mission to Adumar in [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]]. She later joined the New Republic<ref name="Starfighters of Adumar">''[[X-Wing: Starfighters of Adumar]]''</ref> and became a member of Starfighter Command. Her skills with a [[blastsword]] and [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]] eventually earned her a position as head vibroblade instructor for all of Starfighter Command at one point, though she later retired from the service. In 40 ABY, Cheriss ke Hanadi joined Wedge Antilles as part of Rakehell Squadron, flying as '''Rakehell Eight'''. As of the Battle of Centerpoint Station, ke Hanadi was forced to go extravehicular during a dogfight with Rogue Squadron,<ref name="Fury" /> though she is known to have survived the battle.<ref name="Outcast">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Outcast]]''</ref>

===Jaina Solo===
[[File:Jainablue.jpg|thumb|right|160px|[[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]].]]
{{Main|Jaina Solo Fel}}
Jaina Solo, flying as '''Rakehell Nine''' and the leader of Three Flight, was a Jedi Knight and starfighter pilot.<ref name="Fury" /> The daughter of Han and Leia Organa Solo, Jaina Solo was already trained in the Jedi arts and flying in combat against the Yuuzhan Vong at the young age of sixteen.<ref name="NEGTC" /> The war saw her rack up an impressive amount of kills,<ref name="Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand" /> and she earned the label of [[Sword of the Jedi]] for her role on the front lines.<ref name="Fury" /> Solo had prior command experience, both of [[Twin Suns Squadron]] during the Yuuzhan Vong War<ref name="Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream" /> and her last position in the Galactic Alliance military as Rogue Leader.<ref name="Betrayal" /> Leaving the military in 40 ABY over a conflict with her twin brother, Jacen Solo,<ref name="Bloodlines">''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]''</ref> Jaina returned to the Jedi Order full-time and aided in the pursuit of renegade [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Alema Rar]]. Jaina Solo survived the Battle of Centerpoint Station.<ref name="Fury" />

===Zekk===
[[File:Zekk.png|thumb|right|160px|[[Zekk]].]]
{{Main|Zekk}}
Zekk was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] male Jedi Knight who flew as '''Rakehell Ten'''.<ref name="Fury" /> A childhood friend of Jaina Solo, Zekk was once a Dark Jedi before Solo helped redeem him. Since then, he served in the Jedi Order,<ref name="NEGTC" /> including during the Yuuzhan Vong War, which saw him in frequent battle against the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extragalactic]] invaders.<ref name="The Unifying Force" /><ref name="Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream" /> After the end of the war, he was one of the Jedi who participated in the [[Swarm War]] after he, Jaina Solo, and several others became [[Joiner|joined]] with the [[Killik/Legends|Killik]] hives,<ref name="Swarm War">''[[Dark Nest III: The Swarm War]]''</ref> but later recovered and became unjoined. After Jaina Solo's return to the Jedi Order following her departure from the Galactic Alliance military, Zekk, Solo, and Jagged Fel were sent on a prolonged mission to hunt down Alema Rar, whom Fel eventually slew. Shortly after their return, Zekk became part of Rakehell Squadron, participating in and surviving the Battle of Centerpoint Station.<ref name="Fury" />

===Volu Nyth===
{{Main|Volu Nyth}}
Volu Nyth was a female Human from [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]]<ref name="Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream" /> and '''Rakehell Eleven'''<ref name="Fury" /> who flew with Rogue Squadron during the Yuuzhan Vong War. Her involvement included flying in the Battle of Borleias,<ref name="Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream" /> though she retired after the war. However, Nyth joined Rakehell Squadron after its formation by Wedge Antilles and flew in the Battle of Centerpoint Station, surviving her role in that clash.<ref name="Fury" />

===Wes Janson===
[[File:Day9ianliston.jpg|thumb|right|160px|[[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]], Rakehell Twelve.]]
{{Main|Wes Janson/Legends}}
Wes Janson, a Human male from [[Taanab/Legends|Taanab]],<ref name="Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand" /> answered to the callsign of '''Rakehell Twelve'''.<ref name="Fury" /> A veteran of Starfighter Command and a peer of Wedge Antilles and Tycho Celchu,<ref name="Rogue Squadron" /> Janson also served a brief stint as the [[Executive officer/Legends|executive officer]] of Wraith Squadron.<ref name="Wraith Squadron" /> However, much of his military career was spent in Rogue Squadron.<ref name="Rogue Squadron" /><ref name="Wraith Squadron" /><ref name="Starfighters of Adumar" /><ref name="Vision of the Future">''[[Vision of the Future]]''</ref> Janson flew as part of the Rogues until the end of the Galactic Civil War,<ref name="Vision of the Future" /> after which he retired, only to re-emerge during the Yuuzhan Vong War,<ref name="Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand" /> like several of his contemporaries. Once again retired<ref name="The Unifying Force" /> until Wedge Antilles put out the call for Rakehell Squadron, Janson enrolled in the unit, bringing his famous wit with him, and flew in the Battle of Centerpoint Station, which he survived.<ref name="Fury" />

==Behind the scenes==
Rakehell Squadron was created by [[Aaron Allston]] for his third book in the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'' series, ''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]''. The exact fate of the squadron is not given following the Battle of Centerpoint Station.
